data.body:
Goal:
Make `kno ls --json` report internally consistent pagination metadata when a text query returns no rows on the requested page. The observed bug is that, from `/Users/cartine/foolery`, `kno ls -q escalation --all --json --limit 50` and `kno ls -q approval --all --json --limit 50` returned `data: []` while also reporting `total: 50` and `has_more: true`, which makes consumers believe more matching results exist even though the page is empty.

Verification:
1. From `/Users/cartine/foolery`, run `kno ls -q escalation --all --json --limit 50` and confirm empty matches report `data: []`, `total: 0`, and `has_more: false`, unless matching knots actually exist.
2. Run `kno ls -q approval --all --json --limit 50` with the same expectation.
3. Add or update a regression test in the Knots CLI suite that covers a text query with no matching rows and asserts `data`, `total`, and `has_more` agree.

Constraints:
Use `-C` when reproducing across repos. Preserve correct totals for real multi-page result sets; only fix the inconsistent empty-query metadata.
data.title: Fix kno ls JSON totals for empty query pages
